name: ASEAN Charter 2007 (Charter of the Association of Southeast Asian Nations)
summary: The Charter of the Association of Southeast Asian Nations (ASEAN Charter) is a regional supranational meta-framework foundational-ASEAN-treaty framework adopted by the 10 ASEAN member states in Singapore — structurally distinctive globally as the only modern Southeast Asia regional supranational binding-treaty framework operating on formal ASEAN-Way consensus + non-interference framework (ASEAN Charter signed 20 November 2007 in Singapore + effective 15 December 2008 + ASEAN founded 8 August 1967 in Bangkok via Bangkok Declaration — formal foundational ASEAN treaty framework granting legal-personality framework to ASEAN organisation framework + 40-year delayed treaty-form codification framework, distinct from rest-of-modern-regional-treaty frameworks which operate on formal binding-treaty framework from outset, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern regional supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal ASEAN-Way consensus + non-interference framework + only modern regional supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal 40-year delayed treaty-form codification framework), as the central jurisdiction of the formal ASEAN-Way Consensus + Non-Interference framework (ASEAN Charter Article 2 framework providing 'respect for the independence, sovereignty, equality, territorial integrity and national identity of all ASEAN Member States' + 'non-interference in the internal affairs of ASEAN Member States' + 'consultation and consensus' — formal foundational ASEAN-Way framework, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern regional supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal ASEAN-Way consensus + non-interference framework), as the central jurisdiction of the formal AICHR ASEAN Intergovernmental Commission on Human Rights framework (AICHR established 23 October 2009 by ASEAN Foreign Ministers Meeting + ASEAN Human Rights Declaration adopted 18 November 2012 in Phnom Penh Cambodia — formal foundational ASEAN-regional-human-rights framework, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern Southeast Asia regional supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal regional human-rights framework + only modern regional human-rights framework operating without formal individual-petition or court framework), and as the central jurisdiction of the formal ACWC ASEAN Commission on the Promotion and Protection of the Rights of Women and Children framework (ACWC established 7 April 2010 by ASEAN Foreign Ministers Meeting — formal foundational ASEAN-regional-women-children-rights framework, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern Southeast Asia regional supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal regional women + children rights framework with PA-intersection framework). The ASEAN Charter is binding upon 10 ASEAN member states + 1 observer state Timor-Leste pending accession 2025-2026 as of 2026 — comprehensive Southeast Asia regional coverage framework. The Charter is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label, though formal ACWC framework operates substantial intersection with parental-alienation framework via formal Southeast-Asia-regional women + children rights framework. The ASEAN Summit + ASEAN Coordinating Council + ASEAN Community Councils + AICHR + ACWC + ASEAN Secretariat are the formal ASEAN bodies; national-level apex courts operate as judicial bodies. The ASEAN Charter is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label.

statutory_framework.title: ASEAN Charter
statutory_framework.relevance: Charter of the Association of Southeast Asian Nations signed 20 November 2007 in Singapore + effective 15 December 2008 — formal foundational ASEAN treaty framework granting legal-personality framework to ASEAN organisation framework.

statutory_framework.title: ASEAN Declaration (Bangkok Declaration)
statutory_framework.relevance: ASEAN Declaration Bangkok Declaration signed 8 August 1967 in Bangkok Thailand by Indonesia + Malaysia + Philippines + Singapore + Thailand founding ASEAN — predecessor framework to ASEAN Charter.

statutory_framework.title: ASEAN Charter Article 2
statutory_framework.relevance: ASEAN Charter Article 2 framework on ASEAN-Way consensus + non-interference — formal foundational ASEAN-Way framework.

statutory_framework.citation: ASEAN Intergovernmental Commission on Human Rights (AICHR) 2009
statutory_framework.title: AICHR
statutory_framework.relevance: ASEAN Intergovernmental Commission on Human Rights AICHR established 23 October 2009 by ASEAN Foreign Ministers Meeting — formal foundational ASEAN-regional-human-rights framework.

statutory_framework.title: ASEAN Human Rights Declaration
statutory_framework.relevance: ASEAN Human Rights Declaration adopted 18 November 2012 in Phnom Penh Cambodia — formal foundational ASEAN-regional-human-rights declaration framework.

apex_courts.name: ASEAN Summit
apex_courts.role: Formal supreme decision-making body for ASEAN.

apex_courts.name: ASEAN Intergovernmental Commission on Human Rights (AICHR)
ASEAN Commission on the Promotion and Protection of the Rights of Women and Children (ACWC)
apex_courts.role: Formal ASEAN-regional women + children rights commission body.

anonymisation_convention: ASEAN-related communications via AICHR + ACWC framework operate via state-state framework; individual communications not formally accepted under ASEAN framework.

key_developments.title: ASEAN founded
key_developments.description: ASEAN founded 8 August 1967 in Bangkok Thailand via Bangkok Declaration by Indonesia + Malaysia + Philippines + Singapore + Thailand — predecessor framework to ASEAN Charter.

key_developments.title: Treaty of Amity and Cooperation
key_developments.description: Treaty of Amity and Cooperation in Southeast Asia TAC adopted 24 February 1976 in Bali Indonesia — formal foundational ASEAN amity + cooperation framework.

key_developments.title: Southeast Asia Nuclear Weapon-Free Zone Treaty
key_developments.description: Treaty on the Southeast Asia Nuclear Weapon-Free Zone SEANWFZ Treaty adopted 15 December 1995 in Bangkok Thailand — formal foundational ASEAN nuclear-weapon-free-zone framework.

key_developments.title: ASEAN Charter adopted
key_developments.description: ASEAN Charter signed 20 November 2007 in Singapore — formal foundational ASEAN treaty framework adopted.

key_developments.title: ASEAN Charter enters force
key_developments.description: ASEAN Charter entered force 15 December 2008 — formal foundational ASEAN treaty framework operational granting legal-personality framework to ASEAN organisation framework.

key_developments.title: AICHR established
key_developments.description: ASEAN Intergovernmental Commission on Human Rights AICHR established 23 October 2009 by ASEAN Foreign Ministers Meeting — formal foundational ASEAN-regional-human-rights framework operational.

key_developments.title: ACWC established
key_developments.description: ASEAN Commission on the Promotion and Protection of the Rights of Women and Children ACWC established 7 April 2010 by ASEAN Foreign Ministers Meeting — formal foundational ASEAN-regional-women-children-rights framework operational.

key_developments.title: ASEAN Human Rights Declaration adopted
key_developments.description: ASEAN Human Rights Declaration adopted 18 November 2012 in Phnom Penh Cambodia — formal foundational ASEAN-regional-human-rights declaration framework.

key_developments.title: Timor-Leste in-principle ASEAN member
key_developments.description: ASEAN Summit 11 November 2022 in Phnom Penh Cambodia agreed in-principle to admit Timor-Leste as 11th ASEAN member with observer status pending full accession 2025-2026 — formal ASEAN expansion framework.

structural_findings: ASEAN Charter operates a regional supranational binding-treaty framework — places ASEAN Charter in the regional supranational-meta-framework cluster.

Only modern Southeast Asia regional supranational binding-treaty framework operating on formal ASEAN-Way consensus + non-interference framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern regional supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal ASEAN-Way framework + only modern regional supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal 40-year delayed treaty-form codification framework.

Central jurisdiction of formal ASEAN-Way framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern regional supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal ASEAN-Way consensus + non-interference framework.

Central jurisdiction of formal AICHR framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern Southeast Asia regional supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal regional human-rights framework + only modern regional human-rights framework operating without formal individual-petition or court framework.

Central jurisdiction of formal ACWC framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern Southeast Asia regional supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal regional women + children rights framework with PA-intersection framework.

10 ASEAN member state + 1 observer state Timor-Leste pending accession 2025-2026 ratification framework is structurally distinctive globally — comprehensive Southeast Asia regional coverage framework.

1967 Bangkok Declaration + 1976 TAC + 1995 SEANWFZ Treaty + 2007 ASEAN Charter + 2008 entry into force + 2009 AICHR + 2010 ACWC + 2012 ASEAN Human Rights Declaration + 2022 Timor-Leste in-principle ASEAN-framework-development sequence is structurally distinctive globally.

ASEAN Charter + 1967 Bangkok Declaration + AICHR + ACWC + ASEAN Human Rights Declaration formal quintet framework with formal Southeast-Asia-regional women + children rights PA-intersection framework is structurally distinctive globally.
editorial_notes: ASEAN Charter jurisdiction sidecar — regional supranational Southeast-Asia foundational-ASEAN-treaty meta-framework (ASEAN Charter 2007/2008 + Article 2 ASEAN-Way + 1967 Bangkok Declaration predecessor + 1976 TAC + 1995 SEANWFZ + AICHR 2009 + ACWC 2010 + ASEAN Human Rights Declaration 2012 + Timor-Leste in-principle 2022). Only modern Southeast Asia regional supranational binding-treaty framework operating on formal ASEAN-Way consensus + non-interference framework globally + only modern regional supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal 40-year delayed treaty-form codification framework + central jurisdiction of formal AICHR + central jurisdiction of formal ACWC + only modern regional human-rights framework operating without formal individual-petition or court framework + 10 ASEAN member states + 1 observer state Timor-Leste + 1967 Bangkok Declaration + 1976 TAC + 1995 SEANWFZ + 2007 ASEAN Charter + 2008 entry into force + 2009 AICHR + 2010 ACWC + 2012 ASEAN Human Rights Declaration + 2022 Timor-Leste in-principle ASEAN-framework-development sequence.

PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator. NOTE: ACWC framework operates substantial intersection with parental-alienation framework via formal Southeast-Asia-regional women + children rights framework.

Joins regional + supranational + Southeast-Asia-foundational-ASEAN-treaty cluster + ASEAN-Charter-only-Southeast-Asia-regional-supranational-binding-treaty-with-formal-ASEAN-Way-globally-distinctive + Article-2-ASEAN-Way + AICHR-2009 + ACWC-2010 + ASEAN-Human-Rights-Declaration-2012-Phnom-Penh + 10-ASEAN-member-states + Timor-Leste-observer-2022 + 1967-Bangkok-Declaration-predecessor + 1976-TAC + 1995-SEANWFZ + 40-year-delayed-treaty-form-codification + only-modern-regional-human-rights-without-individual-petition-or-court + Southeast-Asia-regional-women-children-rights-PA-intersection clusters within the corpus.
